skill.name: gdpr-compliance
skill.description: Guides implementation and review of GDPR data rights, consent, deletion, export, and audit workflows for Empathy Ledger.
skill.summary: Review GDPR controls for data rights, consent, deletion, export, and audit workflows.
skill.author: Acurioustractor

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown examples and GDPR reference text only. No executable scripts, prompt injection text, data exfiltration, credential access, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited text is Markdown code fencing, API route documentation, and checklist formatting. No executable command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown blocks and documentation examples. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands or evaluate dynamic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: GDPR Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms, but the cited lines describe the skill purpose, GDPR export contents, and compliance checklist items. No cryptographic primitive or hashing implementation is referenced.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text contains GDPR guidance and checklist wording only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Identifiers Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system and network reconnaissance, but the cited lines are data model fields and API documentation for audit access. They do not enumerate hosts, ports, environment details, or system state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious locations are static documentation for anonymization, audit endpoints, and UUID schema fields. No active network probing or system discovery behavior is present.

content.user_title: Review GDPR Controls for Empathy Ledger
content.value_statement: GDPR implementation can miss required user rights, consent records, and deletion workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused privacy reviews for Empathy Ledger.
actual_capabilities: Maps GDPR Articles 15, 16, 17, and 20 to product requirements.
Reviews consent capture, withdrawal, verification, and audit trail needs.
Checks deletion, anonymization, export, and retention workflow coverage.
Provides implementation checklists for data export, deletion, and consent tracking.
Describes API endpoints, database fields, and service methods for GDPR features.
Guides code review questions for data minimization, access, erasure, and security.
limitations: It is not legal advice and does not replace review by qualified counsel.
It is tailored to Empathy Ledger concepts and may need adaptation for other products.
It provides guidance only and does not execute audits or inspect databases by itself.
It does not cover every EU member state rule or sector-specific privacy obligation.

use_cases.title: Review a User Data Export Feature
use_cases.description: Check whether an export flow includes stories, media, profile data, consent records, and activity logs.

use_cases.title: Plan Consent Withdrawal Behavior
use_cases.description: Define what should happen when a user withdraws consent for a story or distribution.

use_cases.title: Audit Deletion and Anonymization Coverage
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether account deletion and story anonymization workflows meet documented GDPR expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic GDPR Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a planned feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Empathy Ledger feature plan for GDPR coverage. Focus on access, correction, deletion, export, consent, and audit requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Consent Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this consent workflow against the GDPR guidance. Identify missing consent capture, verification, withdrawal, notification, and audit trail steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Deletion Workflow Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of erasure and anonymization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this deletion and anonymization design. Check the 30-day workflow, media removal, profile erasure, audit anonymization, and third-party distribution handling.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full GDPR Code Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full GDPR review of this implementation. Organize findings by data collection, consent, access, rectification, erasure, portability, audit, and security.

output_examples.input: Review a new account deletion request flow for Empathy Ledger.
output: The flow should create a deletion request ticket and send user confirmation.
It should define the 30-day processing window and completion status.
It should remove media, erase profile data, and anonymize audit records.
It should notify third-party distributions when relevant.

output_examples.input: Check whether a story consent withdrawal workflow is complete.
output: Record the withdrawal timestamp and update consent status.
Revoke active embed tokens and mark distributions as revoked.
Queue external takedown requests when story content was distributed.
Create audit entries for the consent change.

output_examples.input: Assess a user data export feature for GDPR readiness.
output: Include stories, media files, profile data, consent records, and activity logs.
Use a machine-readable format such as JSON.
Confirm that exported records are limited to the requesting user.

best_practices: Confirm the lawful basis before collecting or sharing story data.
Design deletion, anonymization, and consent withdrawal as auditable workflows.
Keep exports complete, authenticated, and limited to the requesting user.
anti_patterns: Treating consent as permanent after a user withdraws it.
Deleting account records while leaving identifiable media or audit data unchanged.
Adding new personal data fields without a clear purpose and retention rule.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and review prompts. A qualified privacy professional should review legal conclusions.

faq.question: What GDPR rights does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers access, rectification, erasure, data portability, consent management, lawful basis, minimization, retention, and audit review.

faq.question: Can it generate application code?
faq.answer: It can guide code design and review. The skill file itself is documentation and does not include executable implementation files.

faq.question: Is it specific to Empathy Ledger?
faq.answer: Yes. It references Empathy Ledger stories, media, consent records, distributions, and GDPR service concepts.

faq.question: Can it help with data deletion requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines deletion tickets, confirmation messages, processing windows, anonymization, media removal, and third-party notices.

faq.question: Does it cover consent withdrawal?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes withdrawal timestamps, token revocation, distribution revocation, takedown requests, and audit log entries.
